page.title: Paid family and sick leave — Louisiana

hero.headline: Does Louisiana require paid family or sick leave?
hero.subhead: No state mandate. One pregnancy statute. Here's what you actually owe Louisiana employees.

secondary_cta.label: Run the EOR vs entity calculator

answer_capsule.text: Louisiana has no state paid family leave and no state paid sick leave mandate. A 1997 state statute (R.S. 23:642) bars parishes from filling that gap. What Louisiana does have is a pregnancy disability leave law (R.S. 23:341) that applies to employers with 25 or more employees and requires unpaid, job-protected leave up to four months — a threshold 25 employees tighter than federal FMLA's 50.

paid_family_leave.question: Does Louisiana require paid family leave?
paid_family_leave.answer_first: No. Louisiana has no state paid family leave law, and a 1997 statute prohibits parishes from creating one.
paid_family_leave.key_fact: R.S. 23:642 (1997) bars any parish or municipality from enacting paid leave mandates

paid_sick_leave.question: Does Louisiana require paid sick leave?
paid_sick_leave.answer_first: No. Louisiana has no state paid sick leave law, and the same 1997 preemption blocks any local mandate.
paid_sick_leave.key_fact: No state PSL + no local override = employer policy is the only floor

pregnancy_leave.question: What is Louisiana's pregnancy disability leave law?
pregnancy_leave.answer_first: Louisiana R.S. 23:341 applies to employers with 25 or more employees and requires unpaid, job-protected leave for pregnancy disability for a reasonable period not to exceed four months.
pregnancy_leave.key_differentiator: Applies to employers with 25-49 employees not covered by FMLA

fmla.question: How does federal FMLA apply in Louisiana?
fmla.answer_first: Federal FMLA applies in Louisiana for employers with 50 or more employees within 75 miles, providing 12 weeks of unpaid, job-protected leave per rolling 12-month period.
fmla.statute: 29 U.S.C. Chapter 28 (Family and Medical Leave Act)
fmla.employee_eligibility: 12 months employed + 1,250 hours in prior 12 months

federal_protections.question: What federal pregnancy protections apply to all Louisiana employers?
federal_protections.answer_first: Two federal laws apply regardless of Louisiana employer size: the Pregnant Workers Fairness Act (effective June 2023) and the PUMP for Nursing Mothers Act (effective April 2023).
federal_protections.pump_act: Reasonable break time + private space for nursing, all employers

employer_practice.question: What do Louisiana employers offer voluntarily?
employer_practice.answer_first: Louisiana's low payroll burden — a flat 3% state income tax and the US's joint-lowest UI wage base at $7,000 — gives employers budget headroom that most other states don't.

eor_advantage.question: Why use EOR for Louisiana paid-leave compliance?
eor_advantage.answer_first: The complexity is running two simultaneous frameworks (R.S. 23:341 and FMLA) for different employee populations and tracking the 25-employee and 50-employee thresholds as headcount changes.

faq_schema.q: Does Louisiana have paid family leave?
faq_schema.a: No. Louisiana has no state-mandated paid family leave law. Louisiana also prohibits parishes and municipalities from enacting their own paid leave mandates under R.S. 23:642. Federal FMLA provides 12 weeks of unpaid job-protected leave for employers with 50 or more employees.

faq_schema.q: Does Louisiana require paid sick leave?
faq_schema.a: No. Louisiana has no state mandatory paid sick leave law, and a 1997 state statute (R.S. 23:642) bars local governments from passing their own paid sick leave requirements. Any paid sick leave a Louisiana employee receives comes from a voluntary employer policy.

faq_schema.q: What is Louisiana's pregnancy leave law?
faq_schema.a: Louisiana R.S. 23:341 requires employers with 25 or more employees to provide a reasonable period of job-protected leave for pregnancy disability, not to exceed four months. The leave is unpaid. Employees return to the same or a comparable position.

faq_schema.q: Does federal FMLA apply in Louisiana?
faq_schema.a: Yes. The federal Family and Medical Leave Act applies in Louisiana for employers with 50 or more employees within 75 miles. Eligible employees receive 12 weeks of unpaid, job-protected leave per rolling 12-month period.
